soap:
SOAP lance-format/lance#7432

REVIEW · lance-format/lance#7432 — perf: serialize updated_fragment_offsets as RoaringBitmap bytes in proto
jerryjch (infrequent) · M (+180/-8, 2 files) · ⚠ escalated (public-api → full rounds)

ONE-LINER
Adds proto field 10 (`map<uint64, bytes> updated_fragment_offset_bitmaps`) holding per-fragment matched offsets as portable RoaringBitmap bytes, replacing field 9's `map<uint64, UInt32List>` (one uint32/row) that bloated dense-rewrite manifests to multi-GB. Writers emit field 10 only (field 9 forced empty); readers prefer field 10 and fall back to field 9 for pre-change manifests. In-memory type `UpdatedFragmentOffsets(HashMap<u64, RoaringBitmap>)` is unchanged.

FINDINGS (each tied to ground truth)
- Serialize path `transaction.rs:~3530` (patch, `From<&Transaction> for pb::Transaction`): field 9 = `HashMap::new()`; field 10 built by `b.serialize_into(&mut buf)` per fragment, empty bitmaps filtered (`filter(|(_, b)| !b.is_empty())`), whole map `.unwrap_or_default()` when the Option is None. When offsets are None both fields are empty — correct. `.expect("RoaringBitmap serialization cannot fail")` is safe: serialization is into an in-memory `Vec`, so no I/O error is possible (verified `serialize_into` returns `io::Result`).
- Deserialize path `transaction.rs:~3172` (patch): `if !updated_fragment_offset_bitmaps.is_empty()` → decode field 10 via `RoaringBitmap::deserialize_from(bytes.as_slice())`, mapping failures to `Error::invalid_input` (no unwrap); `else` → legacy field 9 via `RoaringBitmap::from_iter(list.values)`. Confirmed `deserialize_from` returns `io::Result` and does not panic on malformed bytes — the error path is sound. Empty→`None` collapse preserved (`if m.is_empty() { None }`).
- Roaring crate / wire format: dependency is `roaring` 0.11.4 (workspace `Cargo.toml:184`), whose `serialize_into` writes the *official Roaring portable* format — a stable, cross-language on-disk format. This is the right choice for a persisted manifest field; not a crate-internal encoding.
- Blast radius of "field 9 always empty": the *sole* in-memory consumer of the offsets is `build_manifest` at `transaction.rs:1943-1958`, which bumps `_row_last_updated_at_version` for matched rows under `RewriteColumns`. No independent protobuf field-9 writer exists anywhere — every other Rust/Python/JNI construction site passes `updated_fragment_offsets: None` and routes serialization through this one changed impl (verified: `python/src/transaction.rs:302`, `java/lance-jni/src/transaction.rs:436/1250`, several `write/*` and `conflict_resolver.rs` sites). So no cross-language writer can be broken by field 9 going empty.
- Wire-compat, forward direction (new writer → old reader): old Lance ignores unknown field 10 and reads field 9, which new commits leave empty → old readers silently recover *no* offsets. PR body scopes this to audit / `readTransaction()` on historical commits, not table data or OCC. That is accurate given the single consumer above (metadata refresh only), but it *is* a real one-way behavior change and the acceptance of it should be explicit.
- Precedence semantics `transaction.rs:~3174`: field-10 preference is *wholesale* (`if !bitmaps.is_empty()`), not a per-fragment merge. If any manifest ever carried field 9 and field 10 covering *disjoint* fragment sets, field 9's fragments would be silently dropped. Not reachable today (this codebase never co-writes both), but the test only covers the same-fragment override case, not disjoint keys.
- Tests (patch, `mod tests`): `test_proto_round_trip_field_10` (write→read, 2 frags incl. offset 0), `test_proto_legacy_field_9_read` (old-writer sim), `test_proto_field_10_takes_precedence_over_field_9` (same key, field 10 wins). Round-trip, legacy fallback, and precedence are covered. NOT covered: a corrupt/invalid field-10 byte blob exercising the new `Error::invalid_input` path; and a large/dense bitmap asserting the size-win the PR exists for.
- Proto field names/types confirmed against `protos/transaction.proto:258/262` and the prost struct field accesses — no mismatch. Field 9 kept (deprecated), field 10 new; no field-number reuse.
- Could not read: none (0 omitted, no truncation).

ASSESSMENT
risk: med · urgency: high — Refined from content: this is an IO-correctness / persisted-wire-format change, which raises it above a path-only baseline; but the blast radius is tightly bounded (single consumer, single serializer, additive proto field, no field-number reuse, robust error handling, no panic path on bad input). It does not reach `high` — there is no data-table or OCC impact, and the one-way old-reader offset loss is confined to historical-txn audit.
escalation: forced on `public-api` → full rounds regardless of cleanliness (routing, not risk). Named and honored.
The change is well-constructed and mirrors the existing `Rewrite.groups`/`old_fragments` migration pattern.

DRAFT COMMENTS
- question (non-blocking): Confirm the "old readers lose offsets on new commits" degradation is acceptable / signed off — the PR says team vote may be needed for the wire change. Acceptance: maintainer/team ack, or a spec note recorded.
- suggestion: Add a decode-error test feeding invalid field-10 bytes to cover the new `Error::invalid_input` branch.
- issue (non-blocking): Wholesale field-10 preference silently drops field-9-only fragments if the two fields ever cover disjoint keys. Not reachable now, but add a one-line assert/comment that they are never co-written, or a disjoint-key test.

RECOMMENDATION: approve (block on 0 conditions) — the open item is a process/sign-off question, not a code defect.
confidence: high · what would make this wrong: if the RoaringBitmap portable format written by `roaring` 0.11 is not guaranteed stable across the crate-version range Lance may deserialize with, or if there is a field-9 writer outside this repo that I could not see — both low-probability given the additive design and the confirmed single serializer.
summary: perf/wire-format change: adds proto field 10 (RoaringBitmap portable bytes) for updated_fragment_offsets, replacing the uint32-per-row field 9 that bloated dense-rewrite manifests. Writers emit field 10 only (field 9 forced empty); readers prefer field 10 with field-9 fallback. Verified: single in-memory consumer (build_manifest, transaction.rs:1943-1958), no independent field-9 writer in any language, additive proto field with no number reuse, decode errors mapped to invalid_input (no panic), and serialize_into's .expect is safe (in-memory Vec). Round-trip / legacy-read / precedence tests present. Escalated on public-api -> full rounds. Net risk med (persisted wire format) but tightly bounded - approving. Open items are non-blocking: a team sign-off question on the one-way old-reader offset loss, a decode-error test, and a defensive note on wholesale field-10 preference.

comments.label: question
comments.body: This is a persisted wire-format change and the description notes a team vote may be needed. New commits leave field 9 empty, so old Lance readers recover no offsets from these transactions (audit/readTransaction only, not table data or OCC). Can you confirm this one-way degradation is signed off by the maintainers before merge? Not gating the code, but the format decision should be explicit.

comments.label: suggestion
comments.body: The new Error::invalid_input branch for a corrupt field-10 blob isn't exercised by any test. Consider adding a case that feeds invalid bytes into updated_fragment_offset_bitmaps and asserts a clean invalid_input error (not a panic) - cheap coverage for the one failure path this PR introduces.

comments.label: issue
comments.body: Field-10 preference is wholesale (`if !updated_fragment_offset_bitmaps.is_empty()`), not a per-fragment merge. If a manifest ever carried field 9 and field 10 covering disjoint fragment keys, field 9's fragments would be silently dropped. This codebase never co-writes both today, so it's not reachable - but a one-line comment stating that invariant (or a disjoint-key test) would keep a future writer from tripping it. Author's call.

comments.label: praise
comments.body: Good discipline here: serialize_into's .expect is genuinely infallible (writing into an in-memory Vec), while the deserialize path correctly maps roaring's io::Result to Error::invalid_input instead of unwrapping - so a corrupt blob yields a clean error rather than a panic.
